message:
perf(btree): balance_nonroot child_page_number uses left-child helper

Fifth call site of the "caller only needs one field from CellRef" pattern
established by commits 4b061dcc, 385591f8, b35f091c, and 145bfe36.

`balance_nonroot` enumerates interior-child pages when gathering siblings
for 3-way rebalance. The per-child `child_page_number` call used full
`CellRef::parse` to read just the 4-byte left-child pointer from each
interior cell — the rowid varint, local_size computation, and overflow
bounds check were all discarded.

Adds `CellRef::read_interior_left_child(page, cell_offset)` in cell.rs:
a 4-byte big-endian read with one bounds check and one non-zero check.
This is the public sibling of `BtCursor::read_child_at_offset` (private
in cursor.rs); putting it on `CellRef` lets both `cursor.rs` and
`balance.rs` share a single implementation.

Rewires `balance::child_page_number`'s `<cell_count` arm to use the
helper. The `usable_size` param stays in the signature because callers
plumb it in from `balance_nonroot`'s outer context.

Measured delta (same microbench pattern as commit 4b061dcc):
  bench_child_page_at_interior_table (128 cells, 5M iters, release):
  direct read = 5.186 ns / call,  CellRef::parse = 28.038 ns / call
  -> 5.41x speedup per interior-child lookup.

Every balance_nonroot invocation enumerates up to 3 siblings × O(cells
per child) interior-child lookups; the split / merge hot path on index
and deep table btrees pays this cost.

Parity: `cargo check -p fsqlite-btree` clean; `cargo clippy --lib
        --no-deps -p fsqlite-btree` clean.
Bead:   bd-ah597 (this is the fifth caller of the shared "one-field"
                  fast-path family)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
